question: My filter effluent turbidity spiked from 0.04 to 0.20 NTU in the last 30 minutes. What should I do first?
reference_answer: Confirm the reading with a bench grab sample. If it holds, check filter run time and headloss. A long run with high headloss points to breakthrough — backwash the filter. If headloss is normal, look upstream at settled water quality and check whether loading changed. Pull the filter offline if turbidity keeps climbing toward your action level.
notes: Tests basic turbidity response reasoning and operator voice.
